title: Sagraea scabrosa
taxonomy: Sagraea scabrosa is classified in the family Rubiaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family comprises approximately 60 to 70 genera and more than 6000 species. The Rubiaceae family is found in almost all parts of the world, with a significant presence in tropical and subtropical regions. Sagraea scabrosa is likely a member of a specific genus within the Rubiaceae family, but further information is needed to confirm its exact classification. uses: Sagraea scabrosa has been reported to have various traditional medicinal uses in different cultures. In some regions, its roots are used to treat fever and rheumatism.
